weather_report:
A soft, persistent rain has washed Wembley this morning, leaving the pavements gleaming and the air smelling faintly of stone and green. Clouds sit low and even, muting the edges of buildings; the sun is a rumor behind the gauze, so the UV stays modest. It’s the sort of late morning that quiets a street, the light cool and pewter, visibility fine but gently blurred by drizzle.

Expect the light rain and on-and-off showers to linger into the early afternoon, perhaps loosening later with a few hesitant brighter spells. Sunshine will be brief when it comes, and the day never quite dazzles. Dress for damp comfort: a light waterproof or trench over easy layers, shoes that don’t mind puddles, an umbrella you actually like carrying. Good weather for ordinary pleasures—picking up a few errands, a slow wander to a café window seat, clearing a drawer, paging through a bookshop, or a short walk between showers to feel the hush of the day. If you’re out long, think of a hat or hood; if you linger indoors, a warm cup will do the rest.
